title: Planning Under Resource Limits
concept: Optimizing plans when time, API tokens, or execution steps are strictly capped.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: when a planning AI gets stuck repeating the same broken step in a loop, a good fallback is to...

options.text: keep retrying the exact same step forever
detect the loop and switch to a backup plan or ask for help
delete all of its memory and start life over

exercises.explain: A smart agent notices when it's stuck in a contradiction or loop and triggers a fallback, like trying a different approach or asking a human. Repeating a broken step forever just wastes effort.

cards.title: Only So Much To Spend
cards.body: Imagine you have 10 minutes, 5 dollars, and 3 shopping trips to buy everything for a party. You can't do it all, so you plan carefully. AI agents face the exact same squeeze with time, money, and steps.

cards.title: What Is a Resource Limit?
cards.body: A resource limit is a strict cap on what an agent can use: time, API tokens (its 'money'), or number of steps. Planning under limits means reaching the goal without going over any cap.

cards.title: A Road Trip With Half a Tank
cards.body: With limited gas, you don't visit every fun stop, you pick the route that reaches the destination. An agent under limits skips nice-to-have steps and keeps only the ones that actually reach the goal.

cards.title: Fewer Searches, Smarter Searches
cards.body: An agent must answer a question using only 3 web searches. Instead of searching random things, it plans the 3 highest-value searches first. Each step has to earn its place.

cards.title: Myth: More Steps = Better Answer
cards.body: Not true! Using every token and step can waste time and even add errors. A tight, well-chosen plan often beats a long, sprawling one.

cards.title: Wasteful vs. Budget-Aware
cards.body: Two agents, same goal, same caps. One burns its budget early; one spends it wisely.

left.text: Does 20 tiny steps, runs out of tokens before finishing.

right.text: Picks the 4 key steps that reach the goal within the cap.

exercises.prompt: An agent is told it may use at most 5 execution steps to book a trip. What is the smartest first move?

options.text: Spend 3 steps exploring unrelated fun facts about the city
Plan which 5 steps matter most, then execute only those
Ignore the limit and take 12 steps anyway
Give up because 5 steps is never enough

exercises.explain: Under a hard step cap, the agent should prioritize: figure out the few steps that truly reach the goal and skip the rest. Wasting steps or ignoring the cap leads to failure.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Using more API tokens always makes an agent's plan better.
exercises.explain: False. Tokens are like money, spending more isn't automatically smarter. A focused plan can reach the goal with fewer tokens and fewer mistakes.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about spending an agent's budget wisely.
exercises.sentence: When time and tokens are capped, an agent should prioritize the steps that actually reach the goal.
exercises.explain: To 'prioritize' means to pick what matters most first. With limited resources, the agent ranks steps by value and does the important ones before it runs out.

exercises.prompt: Which explanation of planning under resource limits was written by an AI?

options.text: just do stuff until it stops i guess lol who counts steps anyway
Under strict caps on time, tokens, or steps, an agent ranks actions by value and executes only the highest-impact ones needed to reach the goal.
my brother said computers never run out of anything so limits are fake

exercises.explain: The AI-written option is clear, complete, and correctly explains prioritizing high-value actions within a cap. The others are casual guesses or plainly wrong.

exercises.prompt: An agent must research and summarize a topic using at most 4 web searches and 1000 tokens. Halfway in, it has used 3 searches and found little useful info. What is the best resource-aware move?

options.text: Use its final search on the single most promising, targeted query, then summarize within the token budget
Keep searching past the 4-search cap until it finds something
Spend all remaining tokens re-reading what it already found, then run out before summarizing
Repeat the same weak search three more times hoping for a different result

exercises.explain: With only one search left, the agent should invest it in the highest-value, most targeted query, then save enough tokens to actually deliver the summary. Planning under resource limits means spending every remaining step and token where it counts most, never blowing past the cap or wasting the budget on repeats.
